extracted_items.content: Project name: taskflow-api — a REST API backend for a task management app
Technology stack: FastAPI (web framework), PostgreSQL (database), SQLAlchemy (ORM), Pydantic v2 (data validation), Alembic (migrations)
User preference: use uv for package management instead of pip or poetry — prefers it for speed
Project structure: app/ (with main.py, models/, schemas/, routers/, services/, db.py), alembic/, tests/, pyproject.toml. Business logic should be in services layer, not route handlers
Testing requirements: pytest with pytest-asyncio and httpx for testing FastAPI endpoints. Minimum 80% test coverage required
Deployment: Docker (multi-stage Dockerfile with uv for deps, slim runtime image) and Fly.io
CI/CD: GitHub Actions pipeline that runs ruff linting, pytest with coverage check, and builds Docker image on main branch
[architecture] Use Pydantic v2 for the benefits of performance improvements, accepting the breaking changes from v1 (e.g., @validator → @field_validator)

extracted_items.supersedes_content: comfygit project is deployed on Heroku
extracted_items.content: comfygit project is deployed on Fly.io (migrated from Heroku last week). Primary region: iad (US East). Singapore (sin) expansion is a future consideration but not yet decided.

extracted_items.supersedes_content: comfygit API is currently on v2
extracted_items.content: comfygit API is on v3 (v2 fully deprecated last month). v2 endpoints return 410 Gone. Migration guide: docs.comfygit.org/migration/v3. Key breaking changes in v3: authentication moved to OAuth2 PKCE, /workflows endpoint renamed to /environments.

extracted_items.supersedes_content: comfygit uses Redis for job queue processing
extracted_items.content: comfygit uses PostgreSQL-backed job queuing with pgqueue Python library (migrated from Redis). Processes ~500 jobs/day. Redis was deemed overkill for current scale.
comfygit database is Neon serverless PostgreSQL (migrated from AWS RDS). Chosen for cost efficiency with variable workload pattern and branching capability for staging environments.
comfygit workflow endpoint is /environments (renamed from /workflows in API v3)

extracted_items.content: Token refresh race condition fix: implement promise-based mutex/lock pattern in React Native app to ensure only one token refresh is in-flight at a time; subsequent refresh requests should return the same pending promise instead of starting new refresh operations
Migrate token storage from AsyncStorage to expo-secure-store for encrypted token persistence in React Native app
Minimum supported iOS version: iOS 16
Minimum supported Android version: API 29 (Android 10)
Project uses Expo framework for React Native development

deduped_items.content: Token refresh race condition fix: implement promise-based mutex/lock pattern in React Native app to ensure only one token refresh is in-flight at a time; subsequent refresh requests should return the same pending promise instead of starting new refresh operations
Migrate token storage from AsyncStorage to expo-secure-store for encrypted token persistence in React Native app
Minimum supported iOS version: iOS 16
Minimum supported Android version: API 29 (Android 10)
Project uses Expo framework for React Native development

issues: Count 5 too high for noisy conversation

extracted_items.content: JWT tokens must use RS256 (asymmetric signing) for this project, not HS256. Public key can be distributed for verification without exposing the private key.
Authentication architecture uses stateless JWT — tokens are verified by signature without server-side session storage.
Auth config requirements: AUTH_PROVIDER is 'internal', sessions last 24 hours, refresh tokens last 30 days, MFA is required, rate limiting is 60 requests/minute.

deduped_items.content: JWT tokens must use RS256 (asymmetric signing) for this project, not HS256. Public key can be distributed for verification without exposing the private key.
Authentication architecture uses stateless JWT — tokens are verified by signature without server-side session storage.
Auth config requirements: AUTH_PROVIDER is 'internal', sessions last 24 hours, refresh tokens last 30 days, MFA is required, rate limiting is 60 requests/minute.

extracted_items.content: Analytics service uses ClickHouse as the database. PostgreSQL was considered but rejected in favor of ClickHouse for its columnar storage and superior performance on aggregations over large datasets.
Analytics data pipeline uses Redpanda for event streaming (Kafka-compatible). Kafka was initially considered but Redpanda was chosen for operational simplicity (single binary, no Zookeeper).
Analytics dashboard uses Grafana, leveraging existing infrastructure monitoring Grafana instance. ClickHouse is integrated via Grafana ClickHouse plugin.
Analytics service handles a few thousand queries per day with complex aggregations. This workload requirement drove the selection of ClickHouse over PostgreSQL.

Analytics service handles a few thousand queries per day with complex aggregations. This workload requirement drove the selection of ClickHouse over PostgreSQL.

issues: PostgreSQL incorrectly extracted as final analytics DB choice

extracted_items.content: CI/CD pipeline uses GitHub Actions with stages: lint → test → build docker image → push to GHCR → deploy to Fly.io. Deployment to Fly.io only occurs on main branch.
Test matrix testing requirement: Python 3.11 and 3.12 must both be supported and validated in CI.
Frontend stack: Next.js 14 with App Router, TypeScript only (no plain JavaScript files allowed).
UI component library: shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS. Do not use MUI or Chakra.
API rate limiting uses tiered per-user limits: 100 requests per minute for free tier, 1000 for paid tier.
Rate limit implementation uses Redis with sliding window algorithm, leveraging existing Redis instance for job queue.
Feature flag naming convention: {team}_{feature_name}_{state}, where state is enabled/disabled/rollout. Example: payments_new_checkout_enabled. Enforced in LaunchDarkly.

deduped_items.content: CI/CD pipeline uses GitHub Actions with stages: lint → test → build docker image → push to GHCR → deploy to Fly.io. Deployment to Fly.io only occurs on main branch.
Test matrix testing requirement: Python 3.11 and 3.12 must both be supported and validated in CI.
Frontend stack: Next.js 14 with App Router, TypeScript only (no plain JavaScript files allowed).
UI component library: shadcn/ui with Tailwind CSS. Do not use MUI or Chakra.
API rate limiting uses tiered per-user limits: 100 requests per minute for free tier, 1000 for paid tier.
Rate limit implementation uses Redis with sliding window algorithm, leveraging existing Redis instance for job queue.
Feature flag naming convention: {team}_{feature_name}_{state}, where state is enabled/disabled/rollout. Example: payments_new_checkout_enabled. Enforced in LaunchDarkly.

scenarios.title: Scenario 8: Very Short Window / Pure Greetings (Edge Case)

extracted_items.content: Architecture: modular monolith. Extract to microservices only when there is clear scaling need justifying the complexity.
API: GraphQL with Apollo Server. Chosen to support multiple clients (web, mobile, third-party integrations) with complex nested query requirements.
Authentication: OAuth2 with PKCE for all clients. JWT tokens using RS256 algorithm. No API key authentication for first-party clients.
Search infrastructure: Elasticsearch. Chosen for complex full-text search requirements; TypeSense deemed too limited.
Caching: Redis for session cache and hot data. TTL configuration: 1 hour default for most keys, 24 hours for user profiles.
Background jobs: Celery with Redis broker. Periodic tasks via Celery Beat. No separate job service to avoid additional service boundary.
File storage: S3-compatible storage using Cloudflare R2. CDN via Cloudflare. AWS S3 explicitly rejected due to egress costs.
Observability: Structured JSON logs shipped to Grafana Loki. Distributed traces via OpenTelemetry to Grafana Tempo. ELK stack rejected as too heavy.
Infrastructure as Code: Terraform for all cloud resources. Manual console changes are prohibited — all infrastructure must be declared in IaC to prevent drift.
Secrets management: HashiCorp Vault for all secrets. No secrets allowed in environment variables directly; all must flow through Vault. This is non-negotiable.
Error tracking: Sentry for error tracking and monitoring across all services.
Code review policy: PRs require 2 approvals before merge. Self-merge on main is prohibited. Main branch is protected. Feature flags required for all risky deployments.

extracted_items.content: Primary GitHub organization is github.com/acme-corp. All project repos are located under this org.
Primary programming language across the org is Python. TypeScript is used for frontend only.
Staging environment: staging.acme-corp.com with separate database. Production environment: acme-corp.com. Never mix or cross-contaminate databases between staging and production.
On-call rotation runs on a 2-week cycle. Alerts are posted to #platform-alerts Slack channel.
Always run the full test suite after making code changes, without waiting for user request. Never skip tests.
Commit messages must follow conventional commits format: type(scope): message. Required types are feat, fix, chore, docs, refactor, test. Always include a scope in parentheses.
Response style: keep responses short and direct. Use bullet points only when listing 3 or more items. Omit preambles.
Code review priority order: security issues first, then correctness, then style. Always check in this sequence.

Code review priority order: security issues first, then correctness, then style. Always check in this sequence.

scenarios.title: Scenario 11: Taste Signal Extraction — UI Corrections

extracted_items.content: [ui] Detail views for secondary objects should use centered modals, not inline expansion
[ui] Prefer compact, dense layouts with reduced whitespace and padding (8-12px on cells/sections) over spacious designs; this is a power-user tool

[architecture] Lists show clean summary data only; all details, progress indicators, and drill-down content go in modals. Apply this pattern consistently across all pages (deployments, targets, environments, etc.)
Deployment list should only display 'Deployed' status badges; hide draft and ready states as these are internal implementation details that shouldn't leak into the UI

Deployment detail modal should include logs with smart auto-scroll: stick to bottom only when user is within 40px of bottom; pause auto-scroll if user manually scrolls up, resume when they scroll back down
Deployment progress indicator should only appear in modal overlay, not inline in table rows; table should show only final state (name, target, status, timestamp)
[ui] Keep current font sizes (14px base is acceptable); focus compression on whitespace and padding instead of aggressive font reduction

scenarios.title: Scenario 12: Taste Signal Extraction — Architecture & Code Review Corrections

extracted_items.content: [architecture] Always check existing utilities and services before adding new dependencies. Do not pull in a new package if existing code can do the job.

Notification service uses specific exception types (NotificationDeliveryError, NotificationConfigError) instead of broad Exception catching. Errors must be surfaced to users via API responses, not just logged.
Notification delivery failures use HTTP 502; configuration errors use HTTP 400.
Notification service implements configurable retry logic with defaults: max_retries=3, base_delay=1.0, backoff_factor=2.0. Retries can be overridden via environment variables or config file.

[api] All API responses must be JSON objects with 'status' as the first field. Never return bare strings or unstructured responses. Success responses include status and relevant data. Error responses use {"status": "error", "error": {"code": "...", "message": "..."}}.
Created utils/responses.py with shared response helpers (success_response(), error_response()) to enforce consistent JSON response structure across all API routers.

[code-style] Test coverage must include every error case and error response code. Tests must assert on full response body structure (JSON format, status field presence, error code/message fields) not just HTTP status codes.
